revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:金崎古墳群 (松江市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55523137]] Wikidata name: Kinzaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55523137]] Wikidata name: Kinzaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Matsue, Shimane, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | native_name =金崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kinzakikofungun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinzaki No.1 | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.48930555555555|N|133.07019444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Matsue|qid=Q207321}}| region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind [[:en:Shimane University|Shimane University]], on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21164 Shimane Prefectural Tourism Federation home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=articles&id=1276&page=1&search_kbn=site&act=&sitename=金崎古墳&sitekana= Archaeological database of Shimane] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:金崎古墳群 (松江市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55523137]] Wikidata name: Kinzaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55523137]] Wikidata name: Kinzaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Matsue, Shimane, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | native_name =金崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kinzakikofungun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinzaki No.1 | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.48930555555555|N|133.07019444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Matsue|WD=Q207321}}| region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind [[:en:Shimane University|Shimane University]], on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21164 Shimane Prefectural Tourism Federation home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=site&org_target=site&subtarget=articles&id=1276&page=1&search_kbn=site&act=&sitename=金崎古墳&sitekana= Archaeological database of Shimane] {{in lang|ja}}

It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:金崎古墳群 (松江市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55523137]] Wikidata name: Kinzaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55523137]] Wikidata name: Kinzaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Matsue, Shimane, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | native_name =金崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kinzakikofungun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinzaki No.1 | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.48930555555555|N|133.07019444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Matsue|WD=Q207321}}| region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind [[:en:Shimane University|Shimane University]], on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind [[:en:Shimane University|Shimane University]], on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].

{{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind [[:en:Shimane University|Shimane University]], on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were excavated, including a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of [[:en:Matsue Station|Matsue Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind {{ill|Shimane University|en|Shimane University|WD=Q1072918|lt=Shimane University}}, on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind {{ill|Shimane University|en|Shimane University|WD=Q1072918|lt=Shimane University}}, on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind {{ill|Shimane University|en|Shimane University|WD=Q1072918|lt=Shimane University}}, on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>

A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.

The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind {{ill|Shimane University|en|Shimane University|WD=Q1072918|lt=Shimane University|1=Shimane University}}, on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|WD=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.

The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|qid=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Kinzaki Kofun cluster !name !type !Size |- | Kinzaki No.1||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 32 meters: anterior: 12 x 14 x 1 meters; posterior 20 x 23.5 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 2|| ''hōfun'' || 10 x 10 x 1 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 3|| ''hōfun'' || 20 x 20 x 2 meters |- | Kinzaki No. 4|| ''hōfun'' || 26.5 x 17 x 3 meters |- | Kinzaki No.5||''zenpō-kōhō-fun''||total length 22 meters: anterior: 5 x 7.5 x 1 meters; posterior 17 x 20 x 2.5 meters |}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:金崎古墳群 (松江市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:金崎古墳群 (松江市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinzaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:金崎古墳群 (松江市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Matsue, Shimane, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | native_name =金崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kinzakikofungun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kinzaki No.1 | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kinzaki Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|35.48930555555555|N|133.07019444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Matsue|qid=Q207321|1=Matsue}}| region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles =; {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}}| architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|29|21.5|N|133|04|12.7|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kinzaki Kofun cluster'''|金崎古墳群| Kinzaki Kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in Nishikawatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|qid=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1957.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139367|title=金崎古墳群|trans-title= Kinzaki Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kinzaki Kofun cluster is located behind {{ill|Shimane University|en|Shimane University|qid=Q1072918|lt=Shimane University|1=Shimane University}}, on a small hill overlooking the lowlands downstream of the Asakumi River to the south. It consists of two "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}), and three {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}) ''kofun''. There were originally 11 Kofuns, but six were destroyed due to the construction of a nearby housing complex in 1963. [[Archaeological excavation]] of Kofun No. 1 in 1947 determined that the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was a pit-style stone chamber made of split stones. A wide variety of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were excavated, including a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with a flower pattern, several ''[[magatama]]'' and several other types of beads, an iron blade, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. The Sue pottery belongs to the oldest style in the San'in region, and there are few unusual examples such as vessel stands, vessels with bells, and vessels with legs.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 3.4 kilometers, or a 40-minute walk, north of {{ill|Matsue Station|en|Matsue Station|qid=Q1058652|lt=Matsue Station|1=Matsue Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.
